# DPR2 - DataPackageR 2

R Packages are the most accessible way of extending the R language.

A **data package** is an R package that primarily provides data rather
than code.

Adding data to a package is as easy as putting data into the `data`
directory of a package’s source. Doing simply that however fails to
inform users how those data were created, a critical component of
reproducible research and information transparency.

DPR2 - DataPackageR 2 - is designed to make data packages that are
reproducible and transparent, and a new implementation of the concepts
and workflows found in the R package .

DPR2 offers:

- source data and processing script storage
- data object and vignette rendering
- data object documentation

The entire process from initializing a new data package, to processing
data, to building the data package for sharing has been wrapped in
convenient functions with a streamlined workflow.

## Working as a team with data processing isolation

DPR2 lets data packages be worked on by teams **developing different
datasets at the same time using git branching**.

Data processing scripts, which are stored in the package source, are
built by **running each script in its own R process** by default, a good
practice when making reproducible data. If desired, a shared processing
environment can be used when configured in the DPR2 yaml configuration
file.

## Data versioning

DPR2 presents a **data digest**, an object that tracks the **md5
checksums** of the data being generated and shared.

When using git with DPR2, DPR2 offers functions that allow users to
**recall data objects** from the git history. This allows users to
easily compare datasets that may have changed throughout the data
package development process.

## Data documentation

Users can access a help file for data objects using the help operator
`?` on the object name,
e.g. [`?mtcars`](https://rdrr.io/r/datasets/mtcars.html).

Like DataPackageR, DPR2 offers a convenient way to generate the
necessary files for making data documentation that can be accessed using
the help operator after the package is built.

# Installation

You can install the development version of DPR2 from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("FredHutch/DPR2")
```

# Examples

For a full explanation of the DPR2 workflow, see the package vignettes.

## Create a data package

Create a new empty data package with then package name “MyAnalysis”:

``` r
DPR2::dpr_create(desc = dpr_description_init(Package = "MyAnalysis"))
```

Create a new data package in the current working directory using the
directory name as the package name:

``` r
DPR2::dpr_init()
```

## Building a data package

Once code and data are added, the package can be built:

``` r
DPR2::dpr_build()
```

# Package index

## All functions

- [`dpr_add_objects()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_add_objects.md)
  : dpr_add_objects
- [`dpr_add_scripts()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_add_scripts.md)
  : dpr_add_scripts
- [`dpr_build()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_build.md)
  : dpr_build
- [`dpr_checksum_files()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_checksum_files.md)
  : dpr_checksum_files
- [`dpr_compare_data_digest()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_compare_data_digest.md)
  : dpr_compare_data_digest
- [`dpr_convert()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_convert.md)
  : dpr_convert
- [`dpr_create()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_create.md)
  : dpr_create
- [`dpr_data_checksums()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_data_checksums.md)
  : dpr_data_checksums
- [`dpr_data_digest()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_data_digest.md)
  : dpr_data_digest
- [`dpr_data_history()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_data_history.md)
  : dpr_data_history
- [`dpr_description_defaults()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_description_defaults.md)
  : dpr_description_defaults
- [`dpr_description_init()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_description_init.md)
  : dpr_description_init
- [`dpr_description_set()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_description_set.md)
  : dpr_description_set
- [`dpr_init()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_init.md)
  : dpr_init
- [`dpr_objects()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_objects.md)
  : dpr_objects
- [`dpr_path()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_path.md)
  : Construct path from data package directory.
- [`dpr_recall_data_versions()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_recall_data_versions.md)
  : dpr_recall_data_version
- [`dpr_render()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_render.md)
  : dpr_render
- [`dpr_rm_objects()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_rm_objects.md)
  : dpr_rm_objects
- [`dpr_rm_scripts()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_rm_scripts.md)
  : dpr_rm_scripts
- [`dpr_save()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_save.md)
  : dpr_save
- [`dpr_scripts()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_scripts.md)
  : dpr_scripts
- [`dpr_yaml_defaults()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_yaml_defaults.md)
  : dpr_yaml_defaults
- [`dpr_yaml_get()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_yaml_get.md)
  : dpr_yaml_get
- [`dpr_yaml_init()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_yaml_init.md)
  : dpr_yaml_init
- [`dpr_yaml_set()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/dpr_yaml_set.md)
  : dpr_yaml_set
- [`project_data_path()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/project_data_path.md)
  : Construct path from data package data directory (deprecated)
- [`project_extdata_path()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/project_extdata_path.md)
  : Construct path from data package inst/extdata directory (deprecated)
- [`project_path()`](https://fredhutch.github.io/DPR2/reference/project_path.md)
  : Construct path from data package directory (deprecated)
